codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of Content::Path with cubic Bezier rendering. The scene graph additions (FillRule, path command constants/helpers, Content::Path variant, SceneWriter::push_path_commands) are clean and well-structured. The render backend implements a complete scanline rasterizer with De Casteljau cubic flattening, i128 overflow protection, 8× vertical oversampling for AA, and both winding/even-odd fill rules. All 12 validation assertions (VAL-PATH-01 through VAL-PATH-09, VAL-CROSS-01 through VAL-CROSS-03) are satisfied by 19 tests. Node remains 96 bytes. No unsafe code introduced. Code follows existing patterns and conventions.

issues.description: Implicit close only handles the LAST contour. When multiple contours lack Close commands (e.g., MoveTo→Lines→MoveTo→Lines with no Close on either), only the final contour gets an implicit closing segment. The first contour's implicit close is never emitted because contour_start_x/y is overwritten by the second MoveTo. Fix: emit an implicit close segment when encountering a new MoveTo while cursor != contour_start. Not exercised by any current test (VAL-PATH-06 tests single unclosed contour, VAL-PATH-07 uses explicit Close). Low practical impact since well-formed paths use Close commands.

Pre-allocating vec![PathSegment{..}; PATH_MAX_SEGMENTS] (4096 × 16 bytes = 64KB) on every path render call. For scenes with many small path nodes, this could add up. Consider using a smaller initial allocation with growth, or a thread-local / reusable buffer. Not a correctness issue — just a performance note for future optimization.

The contribution value 256/8 = 32. Eight subsamples × 32 = 256, but coverage buffer is u8 (max 255). The clamping `if val > 255 { 255 }` handles this correctly so fully-covered pixels end up at 255 as expected. Not a bug, just slightly imprecise — 255/8 = 31.875 would be mathematically exact but integer arithmetic makes this tricky. Acceptable as-is.

f32_to_fp casts f32 to i32 without NaN/infinity guarding. If path data contains NaN coordinates (e.g., from corrupt shared memory), `NaN * 4096.0 as i32` is UB-adjacent in Rust (saturates to 0 or i32::MIN depending on platform). The validation contract explicitly excludes NaN/infinity handling, but worth noting for future hardening.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md library file's Content Type Field Layout section lists only None, Glyphs, Image. After this feature, Content::Path is a fourth variant. The layout section should be updated to include Path: color(4B) + fill_rule(1B) + padding(3B) + DataRef(8B) = 16B.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md lines 7-9 list three content types; system/libraries/scene/lib.rs now has four (None, Image, Path, Glyphs)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md states Node is 72 bytes but the actual compile-time assertion and tests confirm 96 bytes. This pre-existing discrepancy should be corrected.

The path rasterizer uses Vec heap allocations (segments buffer, coverage buffer) inside the render loop. The existing glyph rasterizer in fonts/src/rasterize.rs uses a similar pattern. This is consistent with the codebase but differs from the SurfacePool pattern documented in architecture.md. No convention violation — just noting the pattern for future render backend optimization discussions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/libraries/render/scene_render.rs line 1449: vec![PathSegment{..}; PATH_MAX_SEGMENTS] and line 1616: vec![0u8; cov_size]

summary: Content::Path implementation passes review. The scene graph additions are clean: FillRule enum, 4 path command types with correct byte layouts, Content::Path variant fitting within the existing 96-byte Node, and SceneWriter::push_path_commands with proper 4-byte alignment. The render backend implements a competent scanline rasterizer — De Casteljau recursive subdivision for cubic Bezier flattening with i128 overflow safety, both winding and even-odd fill rules, 8× vertical oversampling for anti-aliased edges, and compositing via the existing draw_coverage path. All 12 fulfilled validation assertions are genuinely satisfied by the 19 new tests. One non-blocking edge case: implicit close only applies to the final contour in multi-contour unclosed paths (not tested, low practical impact). No unsafe code, no wildcard match arms, no regressions. Worker followed the os-worker skill procedure faithfully.
